channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Liam Payne's One Direction bandmates among the mourners at singer's funeral
item.description: The former members of One Direction have reunited for the funeral of bandmate Liam Payne, who died in a fall from a hotel window in Argentina on Oct. 16.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope approves new papal funeral rites to simplify ritual, allow for burial outside the Vatican
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has revised the funeral rites that will be used when he dies, simplifying the rituals to emphasize his role as a mere bishop and allowing for burial outside the Vatican in keeping with his wishes.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel insists on right to act against Hezbollah in any deal to end fighting
item.description: Israel’s defense minister says his country insists on the right to act militarily against Hezbollah in any agreement to end the fighting in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope to make late Italian teenager Carlo Acutis the first millennial and digital saint on April 27
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis said he will canonize Carlo Acutis next April, setting the eagerly awaited date for the late teenager to become the Catholic Church’s first millennial and digital saint.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: Australian communications minister says the government won’t force social media users to hand over personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hong Kong ex-publisher Jimmy Lai testifies he didn't ask Pence, Pompeo to take action against city
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Former Hong Kong publisher Jimmy Lai denied in his landmark national security trial on Wednesday he had asked then U.S.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Higher energy bills push UK inflation to 6-month high in October
item.description: Inflation in the U.K. has risen sharply to a six-month high in October, cementing market expectations that there will be no further cuts in borrowing rates this year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukrainians’ opinions of US leadership falling: Gallup
item.description: Ukrainians' opinions of the U.S. government leadership have fallen to pre-war levels after reaching a high point in 2022, according to a new Gallup poll released on Wednesday. Ukraine's public opinion of the U.S. leadership fluctuated from 20 percent to 40 percent between 2014 and 2021.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — How do you remove children from the harms of social media? Politically the answer appears simple in Australia, but practically the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US defense chief regrets China's decision not to meet during Southeast Asian security talks
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Lloyd Austin has expressed regret that his Chinese counterpart chose not to hold talks with him during meetings of Southeast Asian defense chiefs in Laos, calling it a setback for the entire region.
item.dc:creator: Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lula urges G20 members to take action to slow global warming
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Brazil's president opened the second day of a meeting of the world's 20 major economies Tuesday by calling for more action to slow global warming, saying developed nations must speed up their initiatives to reduce harmful emissions.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, ELÉONORE HUGHES and DAVID BILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: US envoy holds talks in Lebanon on Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire proposal
item.description: U.S. envoy Amos Hochstein held what he called “very constructive talks” with Lebanese officials in Beirut on Tuesday about a possible cease-fire in the Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine fires several US-made longer-range missiles into Russia for the first time
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine fired several American-supplied longer-range missiles into Russia, officials said Tuesday, marking the first time Kyiv used the weapons that way in 1,000 days of war.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin lowers threshold for Russian nuclear weapons use after Biden's Ukraine missile decision
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Tuesday lowered the threshold for nuclear weapons use, just two days after the U.S. authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside of Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: Norwegian mass killer Anders Behring Breivik seeks parole for a second time
item.description: Norwegian mass murderer Anders Behring Breivik, who killed 77 people in a bombing and shooting rampage in 2011, was in court on Tuesday for a parole hearing. It was the far-right extremist's second bid for freedom after spending more than a decade behind bars.
item.dc:creator: OSLO, Norway, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Top House Democrat: Gabbard 'a national security threat' to US
item.description: Rep. Gregory Meeks (D-N.Y.), the ranking member on the House Foreign Affairs Committee, said Monday that former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), now a Republican and President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, is “a national security threat” to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: India has overtaken China as the top source of international students for U.S. colleges, according to a report from Open Doors. For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America, up to 331,602.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Thune threatens International Criminal Court with sanctions if it doesn’t drop Netanyahu warrant for arrest
item.description: Sen. John Thune said he will go after the International Criminal Court with sanctions once the GOP take the majority in the Senate come January if the court does not drop its case against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strike kills Hezbollah spokesman in Beirut; at least 30 dead in Gaza airstrikes
item.description: A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut has killed the chief spokesman for the Hezbollah militant group. It was the latest in targeted killings of senior Hezbollah officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Wreckage of World War II warship found 80 years later
item.description: The USS Edsall, a World War II American warship, was bombed and sank three months after the attack on Pearl Harbor.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: 10 newborn babies die in India after fire rips through hospital neonatal unit
item.description: Ten newborn babies were killed in northern India after a fire swept through a neonatal intensive care unit on Friday; 38 newborns were reportedly rescued from the ward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Moo Deng, Thailand's baby pygmy hippo, gets official song in 4 languages
item.description: In case you can’t get enough of the little pygmy hippo Moo Deng from Thailand, there's now an official song featuring the internet's favorite baby animal — released in four languages for her global fans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US government worker charged with leaking classified documents on Israel's plans to strike Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who worked for the U.S. government has been charged with leaking classified information assessing Israel's earlier plans to attack Iran, according to court papers filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation
